---
title: "LoRa RadioRocket"
description: "Real-time LoRa telemetry for mid- and high-power rocketry: 1 Hz altitude, acceleration, and flight state streamed live to a ground station over 434 MHz. NAR Level 1 certified; high-power deployment on an Apogee Zephyr in progress."
date: "2025-05-09"
categories: [IoT, telemetry, LoRa, hardware, amateur radio, Arduino]
image: ../images/lora-radiorocket.jpg
---

## Overview

A ground-up rewrite of [N3VEM's RadioRocketV2](https://github.com/N3VEM/RadioRocketV2) for the Adafruit Feather M0 + RFM95 platform. Unlike altimeters that store and dump data after recovery, this system streams altitude, G-force, and flight state to the ground in real time at 1 Hz — so you're watching the numbers the moment the rocket leaves the rail. Designed for both mid- and high-power launches; currently flight-tested on a PiercerMini, with high-power deployment planned for an Apogee Zephyr (nose cone e-bay already built).

## Hardware

### TX Sled (rocket)

| Component | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| Adafruit Feather M0 + RFM95 (434 MHz) | Microcontroller + LoRa radio |
| Adafruit BMP390 | Barometric pressure / altitude |
| Adafruit ADXL375 | High-G accelerometer (±200 g) |
| LiPo battery | Power |

### RX Ground Station (APRS Digipeater + LoRa Receiver)

The ground station is a LePotato SBC built into a custom blue acrylic enclosure. It serves double duty: receiving LoRa telemetry from the rocket and acting as a portable APRS digipeater — because most mid-power launch sites are outside range of any APRS I-Gate or digipeater, this station brings the infrastructure to the field.

| Component | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| Libre Computer LePotato SBC | Runs Linux, Direwolf TNC, Node-RED dashboard |
| RTL-SDR V4 dongle | APRS receive + digipeater (144.39 MHz) |
| Adafruit Feather M0 + RFM95 (434 MHz) | LoRa telemetry receiver |
| 12V → 5V DC-DC converter | Field power from vehicle or battery |
| Custom blue acrylic enclosure | Houses all hardware |

**Software stack:** Direwolf (software TNC + digipeater), Xastir (APRS mapping), Node-RED (telemetry dashboard), VNC (headless remote access).

When the rocket eventually carries an APRS tracker (planned: QRPLabs LightAPRS 2.0), this station will digipeat its packets to the APRS network from the field.

## Firmware

Three algorithms do the interesting work:

### 1. Ground pressure calibration

At boot, the sled takes 50 pressure readings over ~2.5 seconds and averages them to establish a stable ground-level reference. Everything else — altitude, apogee — is computed relative to this baseline.

```cpp
// Calibrate ground pressure
float sum = 0;
for (int i = 0; i < 50; i++) {
  bmp.performReading();
  sum += bmp.pressure / 100.0;
  delay(50);
}
groundPressure = sum / 50.0;
```

A simple average works well on a calm pad. A future improvement would be a trimmed mean — discarding the top and bottom few samples — to handle sensor noise spikes at power-on.

### 2. Launch detection

Rather than checking a single axis, launch is detected from the vector magnitude across all three accelerometer axes. This makes detection robust regardless of how the sled is oriented in the airframe.

```cpp
float g = sqrt(event.acceleration.x * event.acceleration.x +
               event.acceleration.y * event.acceleration.y +
               event.acceleration.z * event.acceleration.z) / 9.80665;

if (!inFlight && g > launchThresholdG) {
  inFlight = true;
  launchTime = now;
}
```

### 3. Apogee detection

Apogee is detected purely from barometric altitude — no additional sensors needed. The firmware tracks the running maximum altitude and flags descent the moment altitude stops climbing.

```cpp
if (altitudeFt > maxAltitudeFt) {
  maxAltitudeFt = altitudeFt;
  descending = false;  // still ascending
} else {
  descending = true;   // apogee passed
}
if (g > peakG) peakG = g;  // update peak G
```

Each 1 Hz telemetry packet bundles all of this into a compact string — current altitude, max altitude, live G, peak G, temperature, battery voltage, and flight state — and waits up to 300 ms for an ACK from the ground station before continuing.

## Build Photos

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![TX sled — Feather M0 + BMP390 + ADXL375 in 3D-printed bracket](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/velezf/lora-rocket-telemetry/main/RocketLoRaTelemetry/Feather9x_TX/Tx_Sled/Sled.jpeg)

![Electronics bay](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/velezf/lora-rocket-telemetry/main/RocketLoRaTelemetry/Feather9x_TX/Tx_Sled/Ebay.jpeg)

![PiercerMini — Radio Rocket Edition](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/velezf/lora-rocket-telemetry/main/RocketLoRaTelemetry/Feather9x_TX/Tx_Sled/Rocket.jpeg)

![Ground station interior — Feather M0 + RTL-SDR + DC converter](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/velezf/lora-rocket-telemetry/main/RocketLoRaTelemetry/Feather9x_RX/Rx_BaseStation/Basestationtop.jpeg)
:::

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![Ground station rear panel — ethernet, USB, and power connectors](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/velezf/lora-rocket-telemetry/main/RocketLoRaTelemetry/Feather9x_RX/Rx_BaseStation/Basestationrear.jpeg)

![SCE to AUX — the front panel switch is a replica of the Apollo 12 Emergency Detection System switch. On November 14, 1969, a lightning strike knocked out telemetry 36 seconds after launch. Flight controller John Aaron recognized the signature and called SCE to AUX. Alan Bean found the switch on panel 8 and flipped it, restoring all telemetry and saving the mission. It felt like the right switch for a rocket telemetry ground station.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/velezf/lora-rocket-telemetry/main/RocketLoRaTelemetry/Feather9x_RX/Rx_BaseStation/Basestationfront.jpeg)
:::

::: {layout-ncol=1}
![At the range — ground station connected to laptop, PiercerMini on the table](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/velezf/lora-rocket-telemetry/main/RocketLoRaTelemetry/Feather9x_RX/Rx_BaseStation/RangeSetup.jpeg)
:::

## Status

✅ *Flight tested — telemetry received successfully on first flight.*  
🔧 *High-power deployment in progress — Apogee Zephyr nose cone e-bay built, NAR Level 1 certified.*

## Links

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/velezf/lora-rocket-telemetry)
- [TX Firmware](https://github.com/velezf/lora-rocket-telemetry/tree/main/RocketLoRaTelemetry/Feather9x_TX)
- [RX Firmware](https://github.com/velezf/lora-rocket-telemetry/tree/main/RocketLoRaTelemetry/Feather9x_RX)
